ULR-34: Implement review actions and thread state updates from the chat workspace
description:
Area: Review Flow
Suggested labels: backend, frontend, threads, review

Objective:
Let users request changes or approve directly from the chat-plus-terminal workflow while keeping the same thread as the durable review and execution object.

Scope:

- Implement `threads.request_changes` and `threads.approve`
- Emit review and completion events, update thread snapshots, and surface review-state feedback in the chat and thread panes
- Ensure request-changes and approval actions resolve back into the selected thread coordinator context rather than spawning a new execution object

Acceptance Criteria:

- Requesting changes returns the same thread to `running`
- Approval marks the thread complete according to the product contract
- Review actions are thread-aware and operate against the currently selected sandbox and thread context
- The thread remains the primary control surface for execution follow-up even when review actions are triggered from the main chat

Doc References:

- docs/product-spec.md
- docs/thread-contract.md
- docs/wiring/editor-review-wiring.md

ULR-26: Build thread list, detail surface, and replayable coordinator context
description:
Area: Thread UI / Streaming
Suggested labels: frontend, backend, threads, streaming

Objective:
Make the right side of the Chat page a real execution surface centered on one coordinator conversation per thread rather than a placeholder or raw swarm dashboard.

Scope:

- Build collapsible thread cards for the active project/chat scope, with one expanded thread at a time by default
- Build thread detail with header, status pills, summary, coordinator conversation, timeline, and secondary tabs for `Overview`, `Agents`, `Files`, `Approvals`, and `Logs`
- Implement `threads.list_by_project/list_by_chat/get/get_events/get_messages/get_agents/get_approvals/get_logs`, `threads.send_message`, and replayable `threads.events/messages`
- Wire thread-driven terminal launch: thread detail UI should expose an `Open Terminal` action that calls `sandboxes.set_active` with the thread's sandbox, then `terminal.open` to launch a session targeting that sandbox. Consume the terminal workflow module built in ULR-31 rather than reimplementing.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Thread cards update from snapshots and thread detail rehydrates from persisted data
- Thread events can replay from `from_sequence` after reconnect
- The main interaction surface of a thread is one durable coordinator conversation
- Worker or swarm detail remains secondary under `Agents` or `Logs`
- `Open Terminal` from thread UI focuses the terminal drawer targeting the thread's sandbox

Implementation Notes:

- Keep list queries snapshot-based and timeline/history stream-based
- Thread-driven terminal launch should use the `openTerminal` workflow from ULR-31 and pass the thread's sandboxId for sandbox resolution
- The user talks to the thread coordinator, not directly to raw workers; provider details may be shown as diagnostics but should not replace the coordinator as the stable identity

Doc References:

- docs/thread-contract.md
- docs/ui-layout-and-navigation.md
- docs/wiring/chat-thread-wiring.md

title: ULR-38: Build generic runtime supervisor with restart policy
description:
Area: Runtime Supervision
Suggested labels: backend, runtime, supervisor, processes

Objective:
Create the backend-owned supervision core that starts, monitors, restarts, and degrades runtime components while preserving Ultra's product boundary around Overstory.

Scope:

- Build a generic supervisor for coordinator, `ov watch`, and watchdog child processes
- Implement the documented restart/backoff policy and exhaustion-to-degraded behavior
- Keep runtime supervision behind the coordinator boundary so Ultra continues to expose one coordinator conversation per thread rather than raw worker orchestration

Acceptance Criteria:

- Failed components restart according to the documented bounded policy
- Restart-count and failure-reason state persist and propagate to health views
- Restart-loop exhaustion becomes explicit degraded/down state
- The supervision layer does not expose raw worker-level control as a primary UX surface

Implementation Notes:

- Keep process ownership with Ultra; do not blur worker-level orchestration into this layer
- This ticket is infrastructure for `ov watch`, project coordinators, and watchdogs rather than a user-facing runtime UI ticket

Doc References:

- docs/coordinator-runtime.md
- docs/implementation-plan/04-runtime-supervision-architecture.md

title: ULR-39: Supervise global ov watch
description:
Area: Runtime / ov watch
Suggested labels: backend, runtime, ov-watch

Objective:
Implement the one-global-`ov watch` model the product assumes without surfacing `ov watch` as a primary user-facing concept.

Scope:

- Add a service that ensures exactly one machine-level `ov watch` process exists
- Persist and expose its health independently from project-scoped coordinators
- Integrate `ov watch` supervision into the backend-owned runtime control plane while keeping thread UX centered on coordinator conversations and thread state

Acceptance Criteria:

- The backend enforces single-instance behavior for `ov watch`
- `ov watch` health can be queried and subscribed to
- Failure and recovery updates show up in runtime status surfaces
- `ov watch` remains visible as infrastructure health, not as a direct conversational or control surface

Implementation Notes:

- Keep `project_id` null for the global component record

Doc References:

- docs/coordinator-runtime.md
- docs/implementation-plan/04-runtime-supervision-sprint-plan.md

title: ULR-40: Implement project coordinator lifecycle and thread routing
description:
Area: Runtime / Coordinators
Suggested labels: backend, runtime, coordinator, threads, stdio

Objective:
Turn the resolved one-project-one-coordinator model into a working runtime service built on the backend-owned NDJSON/stdin-stdout coordinator contract.

Scope:

- Ensure a long-lived coordinator child process exists per active project
- Bind coordinator lifecycle to the backend supervisor and the NDJSON stdio transport
- Route `start_thread`, `send_thread_message`, `retry_thread`, and runtime control actions through that coordinator
- Project coordinator events into durable thread and runtime state
- Preserve the product rule that the user interacts with one coordinator conversation per thread while worker fan-out stays behind the coordinator boundary

Acceptance Criteria:

- One active project maps to one long-lived coordinator instance
- Thread identity remains stable across coordinator restarts
- Coordinator routing updates thread and runtime state through durable events and snapshots
- The implementation conforms to the locked command, response, and event contract without introducing side-channel persistence
- Coordinator questions, status updates, and review-ready signals can project cleanly into the thread conversation and timeline

Implementation Notes:

- Separate coordinator identity from thread identity in all state and logs
- Coordinators never write directly to Ultra SQLite; the backend remains the only persistence writer
- Do not expose raw worker orchestration as the primary execution UX

Doc References:

- docs/coordinator-runtime.md
- docs/thread-contract.md
- docs/implementation-plan/04-runtime-supervision-architecture.md
